current_approach_summary: The generator is writing comprehensive, detailed emails with extensive traction metrics, multiple value propositions, and lengthy explanations that far exceed professional cold email standards.

alternatives.strategy: Ultra-brief teaser approach: Write emails under 75 words that create curiosity without revealing full details, focusing on one compelling hook and a simple meeting request. Strip out all metrics, background, and explanations to force brevity.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: Research shows emails between 50-125 words get optimal response rates, and this approach would force compliance with professional length standards.
alternatives.example_signal: A 60-word email that mentions one intriguing fact about the business and asks for a 15-minute call would hit the sweet spot for professional investor outreach.

alternatives.strategy: Bullet-point format with strict word limits: Structure the email as 3-4 bullet points with a maximum of 15 words per bullet, covering only the most essential information (problem, solution, traction, ask). Use white space and formatting to make it scannable.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: This format naturally constrains length while maintaining readability and ensuring the email stays within the 75-125 word professional standard range.
alternatives.example_signal: An email with 4 bullets totaling under 100 words that covers the key points without elaboration would meet length requirements while staying organized.

alternatives.strategy: Question-driven micro-pitch: Lead with a single compelling question related to the investor's portfolio, follow with one sentence about the company, and end with a meeting request. Eliminate all background context, metrics, and explanations.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: This approach forces extreme brevity by using a question format that naturally limits word count while still providing value, fitting well within the 50-125 word professional range.
alternatives.example_signal: A 50-word email starting with a strategic question about their portfolio company's challenges would demonstrate research while staying ultra-concise.

recommendation: Alternative 1 (ultra-brief teaser) because it most directly addresses the core issue of excessive length while maintaining the intrigue needed for investor outreach.

current_approach_summary: The generator is using a detailed, metrics-heavy subject line (55 characters) that gets truncated on mobile devices, burying the key hook in invisible text.

alternatives.strategy: Ultra-short hook strategy: Lead with the most compelling single metric in under 30 characters, using the preheader text to provide additional context. Focus on one powerful number that creates immediate intrigue.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: Mobile notifications show only 30-40 characters, so front-loading the strongest hook ensures full visibility and maximum impact on mobile devices.
alternatives.example_signal: Subject: '$47K MRR, 140% growth' (24 chars) with preheader extending the pitch would be fully visible on all mobile devices.

alternatives.strategy: Question-based curiosity strategy: Replace metrics with an intriguing question that creates urgency and curiosity, keeping it under 35 characters. Use psychological triggers rather than data points.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: Questions naturally engage mobile users who are quickly scanning notifications, and curiosity-driven subject lines perform well in mobile environments where attention spans are shorter.
alternatives.example_signal: Subject: 'Ready for 140% growth?' (22 chars) creates intrigue while staying fully visible on mobile notifications.

alternatives.strategy: Personalized urgency strategy: Use the founder's name and create time-sensitive language in under 30 characters, leveraging mobile users' tendency to respond to personalized, urgent messages.
alternatives.why_it_might_work: Mobile users respond strongly to personalization and urgency cues, and shorter personalized messages have higher open rates on mobile devices.
alternatives.example_signal: Subject: '[Name], 8-week runway' (varies by name length) creates personal urgency while optimizing for mobile character limits.

recommendation: Alternative 1 (ultra-short hook strategy) because it directly addresses the mobile optimization criterion by ensuring full visibility while maintaining the strongest conversion element (impressive metrics).
